meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: API Integration to Fix Deployment Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with failed deployments. Use this expert API integration checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your SaaS projects faster.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures often stem from brittle API configurations and environment mismatches. By automating your API connection layer with Claude Code and validating endpoints via Antigravity, you isolate bottlenecks, standardize environment variables, and ensure your production builds are as stable as your local development environment. Stop debugging, start shipping.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect feature, only to be met by the cold, unyielding wall of a 'Deployment Failed' status. It’s the freelancer’s nightmare: your local environment is pristine, but the moment you push to production, the API calls drop, the secrets vanish, and your client’s confidence begins to erode.

But what if deployment wasn't a gamble? By treating your API integration as a repeatable, automated infrastructure component rather than an afterthought, you reclaim your time and your reputation. This guide bridges the gap between 'it works on my machine' and 'it's live and bulletproof.'

steps.title: Audit API Environment Schema
steps.description: Ensure your environment variables are synchronized across local and production. Use Antigravity to generate a strict Zod schema for all API keys.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript Zod schema to validate all required API environment variables including BASE_URL, API_KEY, and TIMEOUT_SETTINGS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.ts file to validate process.env variables and throw errors on missing keys'

steps.title: Standardize API Client Factory
steps.description: Stop hardcoding fetch requests. Create a centralized factory pattern that handles authentication and global error logging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a reusable API client factory using Axios or Fetch that includes interceptors for auto-attaching Authorization headers and logging 4xx/5xx errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor all API calls into a centralized apiClient.ts factory with built-in error interceptors'

steps.title: Implement Resilient Retry Logic
steps.description: Network flakiness causes most deployment-time API failures. Add exponential backoff to your integration layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add an exponential backoff retry utility to the existing API client factory to handle intermittent 503 and 504 errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement axios-retry or a custom backoff mechanism in the apiClient.ts file'

steps.title: Automate Health Check Endpoints
steps.description: Build a silent 'ping' function that validates API connectivity during the build process before the app goes live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a health-check script that pings the primary API endpoint and exits with code 1 if the connection fails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a scripts/health-check.ts that verifies API connectivity and integrate into package.json build script'

steps.title: Verify Deployment Hooks
steps.description: Ensure your CI/CD pipeline triggers the validation scripts created in the previous steps before finalizing the deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a CI/CD workflow configuration that runs our health-check script before the final production push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update GitHub Actions workflow to run npm run health-check before deploying to production'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated '.env.example' file to prevent 'Missing Variable' errors during deployment.
Implement 'Mock Mode' in your API client to allow frontend development even when the backend is offline.
Log your API latency as a custom metric; slow responses often signal impending deployment timeouts.
Never leak raw API error messages to the client; always map them to user-friendly status codes.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API factory; it ensures your integration survives future refactors.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, verify your production logs for 24 hours to ensure no transient errors persist, then move on to optimizing your API caching strategy.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to eliminate deployment anxiety and deliver high-performance apps that stay online. Don't let technical debt hold your freelance career hostage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
